name: Cloudflare GraphQL Introspection Protection Pack
objective: Detect and prevent GraphQL schema enumeration attempts in production by matching common introspection query patterns in HTTP request bodies.
risk_mitigated: Attackers use GraphQL introspection to enumerate schema types, fields, and operations, accelerating reconnaissance and enabling targeted abuse of sensitive or undocumented API capabilities.
assumptions: GraphQL requests are sent over HTTP and include query payloads in request bodies.
Production environments should generally not allow unauthenticated or broad introspection access.
Teams may require introspection in non-production environments for developer tooling.
tuning_notes: Start in log mode and review for legitimate developer tooling or internal health checks that submit introspection queries.
If your API allows introspection for authenticated administrators, scope an allow rule before this rule using identity, source IP, or environment-specific hostnames.
Constrain expression scope to known GraphQL endpoints (for example, /graphql) to reduce false positives for non-GraphQL traffic.
Retain case-insensitive matching and include whitespace-tolerant patterns for robustness against minor query formatting changes.
deployment_notes: Deploy in log mode first and monitor for at least one full release cycle before switching action to block in production.
For staging or development, either disable this rule pack or add a higher-priority allow rule for approved sources where introspection is required.
After validation, promote to block mode in production zones while keeping explicit non-production exceptions documented and reviewed.
monitoring_guidance: Track hit rate by hostname, path, source ASN/IP, and user agent to identify scanning behavior versus expected internal usage.
Alert on spikes in matched requests, especially distributed attempts across multiple source networks.
Correlate with authentication and API error telemetry to detect follow-on probing or abuse after schema discovery attempts.
Review blocked/logged events during application releases to catch newly introduced legitimate introspection dependencies.

rules.description: Detect GraphQL introspection keywords (__schema, __type) in request bodies.
rules.expression: (http.request.method in {"POST" "GET"} and (http.request.uri.path contains "/graphql") and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "__schema" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "__type"))
